title: cerinthe aspera subsp. alpina
intro: Cerinthe aspera subsp. alpina, also known as alpine honeywort or alpine blue helmet, is a perennial plant species within the borage family (Boraginaceae) characterized by its blue-violet flowers and gray-green foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with cerinthe aspera subsp. alpina include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its essential oil has been used in aromatherapy for its calming effects.
